title: Save Big: Find Free Car Tax Models Still Available UK
metaDescription: Want to cut costs? Explore **free car tax models still available UK** and pay £0 VED. Learn how to find pre-2017 cars that save you money every year. Start your search today!
targetKeyword: free car tax models still available uk
content:
Yes, **free car tax models are still available in the UK**! You can pay absolutely nothing in annual Vehicle Excise Duty (VED) if you buy a car first registered before April 1st, 2017, with very low CO2 emissions. These budget-friendly cars are perfect for cutting your motoring costs. Finding one means checking its registration date and CO2 output carefully. 

Paying £0 in road tax annually is a fantastic saving for any driver. Let's dive into how to identify these vehicles and why they make such smart buys on the used market. 

## Understanding £0 VED Cars in the UK

Before April 1st, 2017, the UK car tax system was mostly based on a vehicle's CO2 emissions. Cars that emitted 100g/km of CO2 or less fell into Band A. This meant they qualified for a grand total of £0 annual VED.

When the rules changed in 2017, a new, flatter tax structure was introduced for all new cars registered from that date onwards. However, here's the brilliant bit: cars registered *before* April 1st, 2017, continue to be taxed under the old system.

This means if you buy a pre-2017 car that originally qualified for £0 VED, it will *continue* to be free to tax. This offers significant long-term savings for as long as you own the vehicle. It's a genuine loophole for budget-conscious drivers.

## How to Find Your £0 VED Car

Spotting one of these money-saving gems is simpler than you might think. Here's what to look for when you're car shopping for **free car tax models still available UK**:

### Check the Registration Date

This is the most crucial step. The car *must* have been registered for the first time before April 1st, 2017. If it's a day later, it will fall under the newer, higher tax system. Always double-check this detail before buying.

### Understand CO2 Emissions

 You need a car with CO2 emissions of 100g/km or less. Many manufacturers made special 'eco' versions to meet this target. These often had smaller engines or specific efficiency tweaks to keep emissions low.

### Verify Specific Models and Engines

It's not just the car model; it's often a particular engine or trim level. For example, a 1.0L petrol engine might qualify, but a 1.2L version of the same car might not. Look closely at the vehicle's specifications.

### Use the Official Vehicle Checker

The government's free online vehicle information checker is your best friend here. Just enter the car's registration number (number plate). It will tell you the exact CO2 emissions and the annual VED cost for that specific vehicle. Always use this to confirm the tax status before purchase.

## Popular Free Car Tax Models Still Available UK (Examples)

While you must always confirm the exact details of any specific vehicle, here are some common models that often had zero-VED variants available before April 2017:

*   **Ford Fiesta 1.0 EcoBoost (99bhp):** A massively popular supermini, certain versions with the 99bhp engine slipped under the 100g/km threshold. They are known for being fun and economical.
*   **Vauxhall Corsa 1.0/1.3 CDTi ecoFLEX:** Many Corsa ecoFLEX models, particularly with smaller petrol or diesel engines, offered £0 VED. These are reliable and a common sight on UK roads.
*   **Volkswagen Polo 1.2 TDI Bluemotion:** VW's answer to efficiency, Bluemotion versions of the Polo are often zero-rated. They combine comfort with impressive fuel economy.
*   **Nissan Qashqai 1.5 dCi:** Surprisingly, some early versions of this family SUV with the 1.5-litre diesel engine also qualified for £0 VED. A great option if you need more space.
*   **Toyota Yaris 1.0 VVT-i / Hybrid:** The Yaris is known for its legendary reliability. Certain petrol and hybrid models (registered before 2017) can be found with £0 VED.
*   **Hyundai i10 1.0:** A fantastic small city car, the 1.0L versions were often in the lowest tax band. Easy to park, cheap to insure, and very economical to run.

## Why Choose a Zero-VED Car from BuyAndScrap.com?

Opting for a car with £0 annual VED offers significant financial benefits. This saving goes straight back into your pocket every year, reducing your overall running costs and making car ownership more affordable.

These older, low-emission cars are often excellent value for money on the used market. You get reliable transport without the hefty price tag or ongoing tax burden. Many also boast impressive fuel economy, adding to your savings.

Remember, BuyAndScrap.com specialises in honest, affordable cars. You can find many suitable low-tax vehicles among our listings. Check out our [Browse Cars](/cars) section to start your search for a zero-VED gem today.

## Frequently Asked Questions About Car Tax

**Q: Will car tax rules change again for older cars?**

A: While the government can change rules at any time, there are currently no plans to alter the VED system for cars registered before April 1st, 2017. These vehicles should continue to enjoy £0 VED if they qualify.

**Q: Do I still need to 'tax' a £0 VED car?**

A: Yes, absolutely. Even if the cost is £0, you must still register the car as taxed with the DVLA annually. Failure to do so can result in fines, just like any other untaxed vehicle. You can do this online using the car's V5C logbook. 

**Q: Does £0 VED mean the car is exempt from MOT?**

A: No. Car tax and MOT (Ministry of Transport) tests are entirely separate requirements. All cars over three years old in the UK must pass an annual MOT. Use our [Free MOT Checker](/mot-checker) to see a car's full MOT history and check its current status.

**Q: Where can I find more guides on buying used cars?**

A: We have lots of helpful information! Visit our [Guides](/guides) section for expert advice on buying, selling, and maintaining your vehicle. If you're looking to sell your current car, you can also use our [Sell Your Car](/sell) page for a hassle-free experience.

Finding a **free car tax model still available UK** is a smart move for any budget-conscious driver. These cars offer genuine savings year after year. With the right knowledge, you can easily discover a reliable, affordable car with £0 VED. Ready to find your next car and save on tax?
